successCriteria.description: File src/shared/types/leave.types.ts exists and exports exactly seven TypeScript enums: LeaveType, LeaveStatus, LeaveRequestStatus, BalanceStatus, NotificationType, AuditAction, EntityType
LeaveType enum has exactly three members: ANNUAL, SICK, EMERGENCY
LeaveStatus enum has exactly five members: DRAFT, SUBMITTED, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED
LeaveRequestStatus enum has exactly five members: DRAFT, SUBMITTED, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED
BalanceStatus enum has exactly three members: ACTIVE, EXHAUSTED, FROZEN
File src/shared/types/index.ts exists and re-exports all seven enums from leave.types.ts via barrel export (export * from './leave.types')
TypeScript compilation succeeds with strict mode (no implicit any, strict null checks) — no type errors in the two created files
The two files have zero imports from other project modules (no dependencies on any other phase)

constraints: All enums must use TypeScript string enum syntax (e.g., ANNUAL = 'ANNUAL') for runtime value stability and serialization safety
No use of `any` type — strict TypeScript with no implicit any and strict null checks
Files must follow the project module structure: src/shared/types/ for shared type definitions, with barrel re-export from index.ts
No dependencies on any other phase — these enums are the foundational layer and must not import from src/modules/
outOfScope: Phase 2 — Leave domain model and repository (src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts, leave.repository.ts)
Phase 3 — LeavePolicy domain model and repository (src/modules/policy/policy.model.ts, policy.repository.ts)
Phase 4 — LeaveBalance domain model and repository (src/modules/balance/balance.model.ts, balance.repository.ts)
Phase 5 — Employee domain model and repository (src/modules/employee/employee.model.ts, employee.repository.ts)
Phase 9 — Leave validation schemas (src/modules/leave/leave.validation.ts with Zod schemas)
Phase 10 — Leave controller and routes (src/modules/leave/leave.controller.ts, leave.routes.ts)

ambiguities.description: NotificationType enum is declared but has no members specified. The intent names it as a canonical enum but does not list its values.
options: Define NotificationType with common leave notification values: LEAVE_SUBMITTED, LEAVE_APPROVED, LEAVE_REJECTED, LEAVE_CANCELLED, BALANCE_EXHAUSTED
Define NotificationType as an empty enum to be populated in later phases
Omit NotificationType from this phase and add it when the notification module is built

ambiguities.description: AuditAction enum is declared but has no members specified. The intent names it as a canonical enum but does not list its values.
options: Define AuditAction with common CRUD audit values: CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, SUBMIT, APPROVE, REJECT, CANCEL
Define AuditAction as an empty enum to be populated in later phases
Omit AuditAction from this phase and add it when the audit module is built

ambiguities.description: EntityType enum is declared but has no members specified. The intent names it as a canonical enum but does not list its values.
options: Define EntityType with values for each domain: LEAVE_REQUEST, LEAVE_POLICY, LEAVE_BALANCE, EMPLOYEE
Define EntityType as an empty enum to be populated in later phases
Omit EntityType from this phase and add it when the audit module is built

ambiguities.description: LeaveStatus and LeaveRequestStatus have identical members (DRAFT, SUBMITTED, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED). It is unclear whether this duplication is intentional or if one should alias/derive from the other.
options: Keep both as independent enums with identical members (intentional separation of concerns)
Make LeaveRequestStatus reference LeaveStatus values via a type alias or re-export
Consolidate into a single LeaveStatus enum and remove LeaveRequestStatus
